1: Because I rejoice in thy salvation.
2: Neither is there is there any rock like our God.
3: And by him actions are weighed.
4: And they that stumbled are girded with strength.
5: And she that hath many children is waxed feeble.
6: He bringeth down to the grave, and bringeth up.
7: He bringeth low, and lifteth up.
8: And he hath set the world upon them.
9: For by strength shall no man prevail.
10: And exalt the horn of his anointed.
11: And Elkanah went to Ramah to his house. And the child did minister unto the LORD LORD before Eli the priest.
12: Now the sons of Eli were were sons of Belial; they knew not the LORD LORD .
13: And the priests' custom with the people was, that was, that , when any man offered sacrifice, the priest's servant came, while the flesh was in seething, with a fleshhook of three teeth in his hand;
14: and he struck it it into the pan, or kettle, or caldron, or pot; all that the fleshhook brought up the priest took for himself. So they did in Shiloh unto all the Israelites that came thither.
15: Also before they burnt the fat, the priest's servant came, and said to the man that sacrificed, Give flesh to roast for the priest; for he will not have sodden flesh of thee, but raw.
16: And if if any man said unto him, Let them not fail to burn the fat presently, and then then take as much as much as thy soul desireth; then he would answer him, Nay Nay ; but thou shalt give it me it me now: and if not, I will take it it by force.
17: Wherefore the sin of the young men was very great before the LORD LORD : for men abhorred the offering of the LORD LORD .
18: But Samuel ministered before the LORD LORD , being being a child, girded with a linen ephod.
19: Moreover his mother made him a little coat, and brought it it to him from year to year, when she came up with her husband to offer the yearly sacrifice.
20: And Eli blessed Elkanah and his wife, and said, The LORD LORD give thee seed of this woman for the loan which is lent to the LORD LORD . And they went unto their own home.
21: And the LORD LORD visited Hannah, so that she conceived, and bare three sons and two daughters. And the child Samuel grew before the LORD LORD .
22: Now Eli was very old, and heard all that his sons did unto all Israel; and how they lay with the women that assembled at at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
23: And he said unto them, Why do ye such things? for I hear of your evil dealings by all this people.
24: Nay, my sons; for it is it is no good report that I hear: ye make the LORD LORD 's people to transgress.
25: If one man sin against another, the judge shall judge him: but if a man sin against the LORD LORD , who shall intreat for him? Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto the voice of their father, because the LORD LORD would slay them.
26: And the child Samuel grew on, and was in favour both with the LORD LORD , and also with men.
27: And there came a man of God unto Eli, and said unto him, Thus saith the LORD LORD , Did I plainly appear unto the house of thy father, when they were in Egypt in Pharaoh's house?
28: And did I choose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be to be my priest, to offer upon mine altar, to burn incense, to wear an ephod before me? and did I give unto the house of thy father all the offerings made by fire of the children of Israel?
29: Wherefore kick ye at my sacrifice and at mine offering, which I have commanded in my in my habitation; and honourest thy sons above me, to make yourselves fat with the chiefest of all the offerings of Israel my people?
30: Wherefore the LORD LORD God of Israel saith, I said indeed that that thy house, and the house of thy father, should walk before me for ever: but now the LORD LORD saith, Be it far from me; for them that honour me I will honour, and they that despise me shall be lightly esteemed.
31: Behold, the days come, that I will cut off thine arm, and the arm of thy father's house, that there shall not be an old man in thine house.
32: And thou shalt see an enemy in my in my habitation, in all the wealth the wealth which God God shall give Israel: and there shall not be an old man in thine house for ever.
33: And the man of thine, whom whom I shall not cut off from mine altar, shall be shall be to consume thine eyes, and to grieve thine heart: and all the increase of thine house shall die in the flower of their age.
34: And this shall be shall be a sign unto thee, that shall come upon thy two sons, on Hophni and Phinehas; in one day they shall die both of them.
35: And I will raise me up a faithful priest, that that shall do according to that that which is is in mine heart and in my mind: and I will build him a sure house; and he shall walk before mine anointed for ever.
36: And it shall come to pass, that that every one that is left in thine house shall come and and crouch to him for a piece of silver and a morsel of bread, and shall say, Put me, I pray thee, into one of the priests' offices, that I may eat a piece of bread.
